source_paper_title: Positivstellensätze and Moment Problems with Universal Quantifiers
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Hu_2025, title={Positivstellensätze and Moment Problems with Universal Quantifiers}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2024.0402}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2024.0402}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Hu, Xiaomeng and Klep, Igor and Nie, Jiawang}, year={2025}, month=may }
context:
Section 7 (Conclusions and Discussions), page 26.

In Theorem 4.6, the condition (4.11) is assumed to hold for all \(l = 1, 2, \dots\). If it holds for only finitely many \(l\), the conclusion of Theorem 4.6 may not hold. It would be interesting to find a finite set of conditions for a truncated multisequence to admit a representing measure supported in \(K\).
title: Finite semidefinite characterization for truncated moment measures with universal quantifiers
background_latex:
Let \(n,m\in\mathbb{N}\). Let \(Q\subseteq \mathbb{R}^m\) be a closed set. Let \(g=(g_1,\dots,g_s)\) be polynomials in two vector variables \(x\in\mathbb{R}^n\) and \(y\in\mathbb{R}^m\), i.e., \(g_j\in\mathbb{R}[x,y]\). Define the (possibly non-semialgebraic) set
\[
K:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: g_j(x,y)\ge 0\ \text{for all}\ y\in Q\ \text{and all}\ j=1,\dots,s\}.
\]
Fix a finite nonnegative Borel measure \(\nu\) on \(\mathbb{R}^m\) with \(\mathrm{supp}(\nu)=Q\). For a polynomial \(f\in\mathbb{R}[x]\), define the quadratic module (a convex cone)
\[
\mathrm{QM}[g,\nu]:=\left\{\sum_{j=0}^s \int \tau_j(x,y) g_j(x,y)\,d\nu(y): \tau_j\in\Sigma^2[x,y]\right\},\qquad g_0:=1,
\]
where \(\Sigma^2[x,y]\) denotes the cone of sum-of-squares polynomials in \((x,y)\).

A truncated multisequence of even degree \(2d\) is a vector \(w=(w_\alpha)_{\alpha\in\mathbb{N}^n,\ |\alpha|\le 2d}\). It defines a truncated Riesz functional \(R_w\) on \(\mathbb{R}[x]_{\le 2d}\) by \(R_w(x^\alpha)=w_\alpha\). A nonnegative Borel measure \(\mu\) on \(\mathbb{R}^n\) is a \(K\)-representing measure for \(w\) if \(w_\alpha=\int x^\alpha\,d\mu(x)\) for all \(|\alpha|\le 2d\) and \(\mathrm{supp}(\mu)\subseteq K\).

For each \(j\in\{0,1,\dots,s\}\), degree parameters \(k\in\{1,\dots,d\}\) and \(l\in\mathbb{N}\), the paper defines a (block) localizing matrix \(L^{(k,l)}_{\nu,g_j}[w]\) representing the quadratic form
\[
R_w\left(\int p(x,y)^2 g_j(x,y)\,d\nu(y)\right)
\]
for polynomials \(p(x,y)\) whose \(x\)-degree is limited so that \(\deg_x(p(x,y)^2 g_j(x,y))\le 2d\), and whose \(y\)-degree is limited by \(l\). The paper’s sufficient condition for existence of a \(K\)-representing measure in its truncated flat-extension theorem assumes the semidefinite constraints
\[
L^{(k,l)}_{\nu,g_j}[w]\succeq 0\quad \text{for all } j=0,1,\dots,s,\ \text{all } k=1,\dots,d,\ \text{and all } l=1,2,\dots.
\]
This is an infinite family of semidefinite constraints in the index \(l\).
problem_definition_latex: Determine whether there exists a finite list of semidefinite constraints (i.e., requiring \(L^{(k,l)}_{\nu,g_j}[w]\succeq 0\) only for finitely many values of \(l\), possibly depending on \(d\), \(g\), \(Q\), and \(\nu\)) that is necessary and sufficient for a truncated multisequence \(w=(w_\alpha)_{|\alpha|\le 2d}\) to admit a \(K\)-representing measure \(\mu\) (equivalently, a representing measure supported in \(K\)).
latex:
Let \(n,m\in\mathbb{N}\). Let \(Q\subseteq \mathbb{R}^m\) be a closed set. Let \(g=(g_1,\dots,g_s)\) be polynomials in two vector variables \(x\in\mathbb{R}^n\) and \(y\in\mathbb{R}^m\), i.e., \(g_j\in\mathbb{R}[x,y]\). Define the (possibly non-semialgebraic) set
\[
K:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: g_j(x,y)\ge 0\ \text{for all}\ y\in Q\ \text{and all}\ j=1,\dots,s\}.
\]
Fix a finite nonnegative Borel measure \(\nu\) on \(\mathbb{R}^m\) with \(\mathrm{supp}(\nu)=Q\). For a polynomial \(f\in\mathbb{R}[x]\), define the quadratic module (a convex cone)
\[
\mathrm{QM}[g,\nu]:=\left\{\sum_{j=0}^s \int \tau_j(x,y) g_j(x,y)\,d\nu(y): \tau_j\in\Sigma^2[x,y]\right\},\qquad g_0:=1,
\]
where \(\Sigma^2[x,y]\) denotes the cone of sum-of-squares polynomials in \((x,y)\).

A truncated multisequence of even degree \(2d\) is a vector \(w=(w_\alpha)_{\alpha\in\mathbb{N}^n,\ |\alpha|\le 2d}\). It defines a truncated Riesz functional \(R_w\) on \(\mathbb{R}[x]_{\le 2d}\) by \(R_w(x^\alpha)=w_\alpha\). A nonnegative Borel measure \(\mu\) on \(\mathbb{R}^n\) is a \(K\)-representing measure for \(w\) if \(w_\alpha=\int x^\alpha\,d\mu(x)\) for all \(|\alpha|\le 2d\) and \(\mathrm{supp}(\mu)\subseteq K\).

For each \(j\in\{0,1,\dots,s\}\), degree parameters \(k\in\{1,\dots,d\}\) and \(l\in\mathbb{N}\), the paper defines a (block) localizing matrix \(L^{(k,l)}_{\nu,g_j}[w]\) representing the quadratic form
\[
R_w\left(\int p(x,y)^2 g_j(x,y)\,d\nu(y)\right)
\]
for polynomials \(p(x,y)\) whose \(x\)-degree is limited so that \(\deg_x(p(x,y)^2 g_j(x,y))\le 2d\), and whose \(y\)-degree is limited by \(l\). The paper’s sufficient condition for existence of a \(K\)-representing measure in its truncated flat-extension theorem assumes the semidefinite constraints
\[
L^{(k,l)}_{\nu,g_j}[w]\succeq 0\quad \text{for all } j=0,1,\dots,s,\ \text{all } k=1,\dots,d,\ \text{and all } l=1,2,\dots.
\]
This is an infinite family of semidefinite constraints in the index \(l\).

Determine whether there exists a finite list of semidefinite constraints (i.e., requiring \(L^{(k,l)}_{\nu,g_j}[w]\succeq 0\) only for finitely many values of \(l\), possibly depending on \(d\), \(g\), \(Q\), and \(\nu\)) that is necessary and sufficient for a truncated multisequence \(w=(w_\alpha)_{|\alpha|\le 2d}\) to admit a \(K\)-representing measure \(\mu\) (equivalently, a representing measure supported in \(K\)).
open_problem_quote: In Theorem 4.6, the condition (4.11) is assumed to hold for all \(l = 1, 2, \dots\). If it holds for only finitely many \(l\), the conclusion of Theorem 4.6 may not hold. It would be interesting to find a finite set of conditions for a truncated multisequence to admit a representing measure supported in \(K\).
subject_classification: real algebraic geometry and moment problems
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can algorithmically search for truncated sequences \(w\) and test feasibility of finitely many SDP constraints versus existence of a representing measure (e.g., via flat extension or by extracting atoms when possible), but the space of candidate \(w\) and of quantifier-defined sets \(K\) is large and the existence of a \(K\)-measure is itself nontrivial to certify computationally in general.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: The goal is a theoretical characterization (a finite necessary-and-sufficient condition) rather than an explicit constructive algorithm with a clear, efficiently computable fitness signal. Automated evaluation of proposed characterizations would require proof checking across all instances, which is not readily reducible to scalable computational tests.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
